title: Explicitly set default CNI network name in libpod.conf
body: Makes Podman network selection more deterministic in cases where multiple CNI networks are present. Should resolve issues with our config file being overridden by the CRI-O config file.

comments.body: @vrothberg If you're not shipping the CNI config we include in this repo (`87-podman-bridge.conflist`) you might want to comment this out by default
Looks like flakes in the tests.
How/where is the conf variable being used/parsed?
We load the conf when the libpod Runtime initializes. Anything set in the config file overrides the default config, and is overridden by user-specified options
(The default config being the defaults baked into `runtime.go` - I chose not to put this there because the current empty string default is more multi-distro safe)
